red_baseline.reason: T023 proves shared lifetime/takeover and isolates incompatible runtime authorization to MCP compatibility propagation.
red_baseline.summary: 2/3 pass; incompatible observed component identity still reports canWrite=true.

validation.summary: 3/3 real adapter-exit, compatible takeover, and incompatible diagnosis/write-refusal journeys pass.
69/69 focused compatibility, recovery, ownership, lifecycle, CLI control-plane, and real stdio tests pass.
Syntax check passes for 98 JavaScript files; whitespace validation reports no errors.

264 total, 263 passed, 1 existing POSIX skip, 0 failures; the first run exposed only test discovery of the proxy helper, and the original affected validations passed before the unchanged full command was rerun.

review.evidence: One assessed compatibility value feeds capabilities, diagnostics, provenance, and Kernel policy; incompatible writes stop before effects, compatible adapters retain one shared state/run authority, and no Plugin-owned process or migration path was added.

obligation_evidence.evidence: CLI and MCP continue to manage one shared Kernel/control-plane authority.
Independent version axes and component digests produce the write decision.
Adapter exit and replacement leave managed resources and LAUNCHDECK_HOME intact.
Observed component mismatch is exposed in capabilities/diagnosis and task.stop returns compatibility_mismatch.
Compatibility remains digest/axis-based rather than package-version/path-based.
Replacement performs no migration, pruning, or package-lifecycle state mutation.
Closing MCP leaves the same task live and observable.
Shared registry, state, logs, and spawn evidence survive replacement.
MCP shutdown never signals the managed service.
Compatible Codex/Claude identities reuse one run/PID without respawn.
Standalone CLI observes and safely stops the surviving run.

recovery: The first full suite discovered the response-loss proxy helper as a test and failed on its missing proxy-only environment. The main guard now activates only for explicit fault-proxy launches; helper, response-loss, original takeover, and unchanged full-suite commands all passed afterward.
